behavior_summary: U8P_STALEGG::hatch implements an item-centric hatching sequence: it checks whether the egg has already been marked hatched, marks it as hatched, plays camera and sound feedback, spawns a helper process, creates and pops a new item at the egg's coordinates, sets a status bit on that item, and launches an Item::hurl process to animate/throw the created item.
U8P_STALEGG::leaveFastArea is a small cleanup handler that resets this item's quality to 0 when the actor/item leaves a fast-area trigger region.
notable_control_flow: Guard: U8P_STALEGG::hatch only proceeds with the full sequence when Egg::getEggId(this) != 0x01 (prevents repeated hatching).

Synchronized child processes: U8P_STALEGG::hatch spawns another process (raw spawn ->FREE::func1C19) and later starts/suspends for an Item::hurl process — indicating the script waits for those child processes to progress/complete at points.
Linear sequence of create->pop->orStatus->hurl for the produced item: creation, placement at egg coordinates, status flag OR, then animated launch.
open_questions: What specific entity or behaviour is created by the raw spawn (spawn ->FREE::func1C19)? The spawn target and its purpose are inferred but not explicit.
What concrete game object does item id 0x014A refer to (the created/pop'd item)?
What exact gameplay meaning does the status bit 0x02 have when OR'd onto the created item?
What triggers the U8P_STALEGG::hatch process in-game (timed, use, damage, or external trigger) — the excerpt shows the process but not the entry event.

main_function.name: U8P_STALEGG::hatch
main_function.purpose: Perform the egg hatching sequence: mark egg as hatched, provide camera and audio feedback, spawn/coordinate helper processes, create and place a produced item at the egg's position, set its status, and animate/hurl it.
entry_conditions: Invoked as a process (processType 0x0210) — likely started by game logic when an egg should U8P_STALEGG::hatch.
Runtime guard: Egg::getEggId(this) != 0x01 (only run full sequence if egg is not already marked hatched).

function_map.name: U8P_STALEGG::hatch
function_map.role: Main hatching process for the egg/item; coordinates state change, effects, spawning, and item creation/animation.

function_map.name: U8P_STALEGG::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit handler that clears/resets this item's quality field (Item::setQuality(0x00,this)).

function_map.name: Egg::getEggId
function_map.role: Query egg state/id to decide whether to run U8P_STALEGG::hatch sequence (used as guard).

function_map.name: Egg::setEggId
function_map.role: Mark egg state/id (called with 0x01 to record hatched/processed).

function_map.name: Camera::startQuake
function_map.role: Begin camera quake effect used during U8P_STALEGG::hatch sequence.

function_map.name: Camera::stopQuake
function_map.role: End camera quake effect after sequence.

function_map.name: spawn ->FREE::func1C19
function_map.role: Spawn a helper/creature process as part of hatching (target inferred, low-level name preserved).

function_map.name: Item::create
function_map.role: Allocate/create an item instance returned into a local pointer (used for the hatched object).

function_map.name: Item::pop
function_map.role: Place/pop the created item into the world at egg coordinates (uses Item::getX/Item::getY on the egg).

function_map.name: Item::orStatus
function_map.role: Set/OR status flags on the created item (OR with 0x02 observed).

function_map.name: Item::hurl
function_map.role: Process that animates/hurls the created item; U8P_STALEGG::hatch starts this process and waits on it.

function_map.name: Item::setQuality
function_map.role: Set this item's quality field; used by U8P_STALEGG::leaveFastArea to reset quality to 0.

external_calls.purpose: Guard: check whether egg is already marked hatched (compare to 0x01).
Mark the egg as hatched (sets id/state to 0x01 to prevent repeat).
Start a camera quake effect to emphasize the hatching event.
Play a sound effect associated with hatching (IDs 0x00C8,0x31 observed).
Spawn a helper/creature process as part of the hatching; return stored in a temporary/pid and script synchronizes with it.
Allocate/create the hatched item's instance and store pointer in local1.
Place the created item into the world at the egg's X/Y coordinates.
Set a status flag (OR with 0x02) on the created item after popping it.
Stop the camera quake effect after the sequence steps complete.
Start an animation/process to hurl the created item; U8P_STALEGG::hatch waits/suspends for it.
Reset this item's quality to 0 as cleanup when leaving the fast-area trigger.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_STALEGG::hatch implements an item-centric hatching sequence: it checks whether the egg has already been marked hatched, marks it as hatched, plays camera and sound feedback, spawns a helper process, creates and pops a new item at the egg's coordinates, sets a status bit on that item, and launches an Item::hurl process to animate/throw the created item.
